QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_38

question.text: When aggregate open interest in a stock derivative reaches 95% of the Market Wide Position Limit:

answer.text: Only square-off/closing transactions are permitted; no new positions can be created
Trading in that stock derivative is halted completely

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** When open interest reaches 95% of MWPL, the exchange restricts trading to only square-off (closing) transactions. No fresh/new positions are allowed. This prevents the position limit from being breached. Normal trading resumes when OI falls below 80% of MWPL.
**The Trap:** Trading is not completely halted - square-off transactions are still allowed. The trigger is 95% (not 100%). The restriction is lifted at 80% (not when OI drops below 95%).
**Cross-Exam Context:** The 95% trigger and 80% release thresholds are specific factual details tested in exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 95% MWPL: no new positions, only square-off. Resumes at 80%. Prevents position limit breach.
